title: Login Initiation for Pooled Path with Tenant Discovery
story: As a DataTools Pro user on a shared/pooled host, I want to log in by selecting my tenant or entering my email, so that the system routes me to the correct organization even without a dedicated subdomain.
description:
Users arriving at a shared/pooled host need a way to identify their tenant before authentication can proceed. The system must resolve the correct tenant and route the user through the appropriate auth flow.

## Flow

1. User visits pooled host (e.g., `app.us1.datatoolspro.com`)
2. User selects tenant slug or enters email address
3. System resolves the correct tenant/org from the input
4. User is routed through authentication for their organization
5. User returns to the pooled host with a session scoped to their org
acceptance_criteria: Pooled host presents a tenant selection or email entry interface
System resolves tenant from slug or email domain
User is routed through the correct Stytch Organization auth flow
Post-login session is scoped to the resolved organization
Invalid tenant selections show a clear error message
ux_changes: Tenant discovery interface on the pooled login page — user selects their organization from a list or enters their email address to resolve their tenant.
middleware_changes: Tenant resolution logic that maps user input (slug or email domain) to the correct Stytch Organization and initiates the appropriate auth flow.
implementation_notes: Email-based tenant resolution requires mapping email domains to tenant organizations. Slug-based resolution uses the tenant directory directly. Both paths converge on the same Stytch Organization auth flow.
